body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription` **Service directory:** `sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/` **Triggered by:** commit 6d10a352b (#58040) by @jsquire ## What Changed The push introduced the `Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription` package (including all sample test files) and specifically marked `TranscribeWithPhraseList` tests as `[Ignore]` due to flakiness. The package ships with nine sample test files each containing `#region Snippet:` markers, and the README references those snippets via `` ```C# Snippet: `` placeholders ŌĆö **except** for the multilingual transcription feature (`Sample09_MultilingualTranscription.cs`). ## Gaps Found - **Missing Examples section entry:** The README `## Examples` table-of-contents (and body) covers 9 features but omits multilingual transcription. There is no `### Transcribe multilingual audio` heading or example code block anywhere in the README body. - **Orphaned snippet:** `sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/tests/Samples/Sample09_MultilingualTranscription.cs` defines `#region Snippet:TranscribeWithMultilingualModel` / `#endregion`, but no matching `` ```C# Snippet:TranscribeWithMultilingualModel `` placeholder exists in the README. The `Update-Snippets.ps1` script therefore never injects this code into the README. - **No preview notice:** `sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/samples/Sample_9_MultilingualTranscription.md` explicitly labels the feature _"Multilingual Transcription (Preview)"_ and notes that the multilingual model outputs a "major locale" (e.g., always `en-US` for English regardless of accent). Neither the preview status nor the major-locale behavior is documented in the README's **Key concepts** or **Examples** sections. - **Supported locales not listed:** The standalone sample doc lists 14 supported locales (`de-DE, en-AU, en-CA, en-GB, en-IN, en-US, es-ES, es-MX, fr-CA, fr-FR, it-IT, ja-JP, ko-KR, zh-CN`); the README does not.
­¤ōÉ Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/README.md` ŌĆö **edit** 1. Add `- [Transcribe multilingual audio (preview)](#transcribe-multilingual-audio-preview)` to the Examples table-of-contents after the "Transcribe with a known language" bullet (around line 150). 2. Add the following new section to the **Examples** body, after the existing `### Transcribe with a known language` section and before `### Use Enhanced Mode for highest accuracy`: ````markdown ### Transcribe multilingual audio (preview) When your audio contains speech that switches between languages, you can use the multilingual transcription model by **not specifying any locales**. The service automatically detects and transcribes each language segment. > [!NOTE] > Multilingual transcription is currently in **preview**. The model reports a "major locale" per phrase (for example, always `en-US` for English regardless of accent). Supported locales: `de-DE`, `en-AU`, `en-CA`, `en-GB`, `en-IN`, `en-US`, `es-ES`, `es-MX`, `fr-CA`, `fr-FR`, `it-IT`, `ja-JP`, `ko-KR`, `zh-CN`. For more information, see [Fast transcription - Multilingual transcription](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/ai-services/speech-service/fast-transcription-create?tabs=multilingual-transcription-on). ```C# Snippet:TranscribeWithMultilingualModel ``` ```` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The snippet `TranscribeWithMultilingualModel` already exists in the test file. No new test code is needed. - **Test file:** `sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/tests/Samples/Sample09_MultilingualTranscription.cs` - The `#region Snippet:TranscribeWithMultilingualModel` / `#endregion` is already present; no changes needed. - **README location:** Insert the placeholder after the preview note in the new `### Transcribe multilingual audio (preview)` section added in Step 1. - **Snippet name:** `TranscribeWithMultilingualModel` ŌĆö must match exactly. After inserting the placeholder, run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 transcription` to inject the code automatically. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all required sections in the correct order: 1. Ō£ģ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client) 2. Ō£ģ Key concepts 3. ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Examples ŌĆö **missing** the multilingual transcription entry (fix in Step 1) 4. Ō£ģ Troubleshooting 5. Ō£ģ Next steps 6. Ō£ģ Contributing No other structural changes are needed. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order from the repository root. Each must succeed before proceeding: 1. `dotnet build sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 transcription` ŌĆö injects `Snippet:TranscribeWithMultilingualModel` into the README. Verify with `git diff sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/README.md` that the code block was populated. 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 transcription` ŌĆö only needed if public API surface changed; no public API changes are expected for this doc-only update. 5. `dotnet format sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/` ŌĆö ensures consistent code formatting.
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [Transcription] Docs: README missing multilingual transcription example and preview notice
